title: monotonicity check in Rust, not SQL — correct given TEXT storage
description: A contributor might suggest replacing the Rust read-compare-write with a SQL max() or WHERE clause. Do not do this. diversifier_index is stored as TEXT to avoid SQLite's signed i64 overflow on large u128 values. SQLite TEXT comparison is lexicographic: max('200','50') = '50' because '5' > '2'. A SQL-based monotonicity guard would silently pass retrograde writes for multi-digit values with a larger leading digit. The Rust-level check is correct and the code comment explains why. This is not premature optimization; it is the only correct approach given the storage type.
close_reason: decision records, no code change needed
